---
title: "Trains"
author: "Leo Katz"
execute:
  echo: true
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(primer.data)
```

```{r}
glimpse(trains)
```

## Summary

Attitudes toward immigration are related to brief encounters with speakers of another language in daily life, at least somewhat. Using data from Ryan Enos’s January 2012 randomized experiment on Boston-area Metra platforms (115 commuters, randomly assigned to exposure or non-exposure to Spanish-speaking confederates for two weeks), we estimate the average causal effect of that exposure on anti-immigration attitudes.
